metadata.source: Ministry of Industry and Information Technology, China Machinery Industry Federation, National Bureau of Statistics

industry_overview.title: China Industrial Machinery Industry Overview (2026)
industry_overview.summary: Industrial machinery is the backbone of China's manufacturing sector, encompassing machine tools, construction machinery, agricultural equipment, textile machinery, packaging lines, and industrial robots. In 2026, the industry is projected to exceed ¥11.8 trillion in revenue, driven by smart manufacturing upgrades, equipment replacement cycles, green transformation, and Belt & Road exports.

key_policy_initiatives.name: Action Plan for High-End Equipment Manufacturing Innovation (2024-2026)
key_measures: Establish 20 national manufacturing innovation centers for key machinery sectors
Achieve 75% self-sufficiency rate in core components (hydraulics, controllers, precision spindles) by 2026
Promote digitalization and intelligent manufacturing in machinery plants
Support cross-border M&A for advanced servo drives and control systems
Strengthen supply chain resilience through localized sourcing of castings and forgings

current_status_2025.automation_rate: 62% (assembly and testing lines in large enterprises)

upstream.description: Raw materials, key components, and design/ simulation software

subsectors: metal cutting machine tools (CNC lathes, milling, grinding)

key_hubs.specialization: High-end machine tools, robotics R&D, automation system integration
key_hubs.supplier_example: Shanghai Electric Group – provides complete production lines for automotive and aerospace, ISO 9001/14001, annual revenue >$8B

key_hubs.specialization: Precision machining, linear guides, ball screws
key_hubs.supplier_example: Suzhou Hannover Precision Machinery Co., Ltd. – ISO 9001 certified, supplies THK-alternative linear motion components, 50k units/month
Wuxi Hengli Hydraulic Co., Ltd. – leading domestic hydraulic pump manufacturer, supplies SANY, XCMG, A-grade supplier status

strengths: Strongest R&D talent pool and university collaboration
Mature supply chain for precision parts
Proximity to major automotive and electronics OEMs

key_hubs.specialization: Industrial robots, CNC controllers, automation lines
key_hubs.supplier_example: Inovance Technology – leading domestic servo drive and PLC supplier, IEC 61131-3 compliant, 200k+ systems shipped
Dongguan Yizumi Precision Machinery Co., Ltd. – all-electric injection molding machines, CE certified, export to 80+ countries
Foshan Coretamp Packaging Machinery Co., Ltd. – provides turnkey liquid filling lines, 500+ installations worldwide

strengths: Strong integration with consumer electronics and 3C industries
Highly flexible small-to-medium batch production capabilities

key_hubs.supplier_example: Qingdao Saigaoke Agricultural Equipment Co., Ltd. – ISO 9001, produces corn harvesters and seeders, 3000 units/year
Yantai Eddie Precision Machinery Co., Ltd. – ISO 9001, supplies hydraulic breakers to global OEMs, 2000 units/month

strengths: Strong government support for agricultural modernization

specializations: heavy-duty machine tools (large lathes, boring mills)

key_hubs.supplier_example: Shenyang Machine Tool Co., Ltd. – one of China's oldest machine tool builders, ISO 9001, supplies aerospace and automotive industries
Dalian Huarui Heavy Industry Group – cranes, bulk material handling equipment, used in ports and mining

strengths: Deep industrial heritage and skilled workforce
State-owned enterprise support for major projects

key_hubs.supplier_example: Chengdu Forward Technology Corporation – supplies precision parts for commercial aircraft, AS9100D certified
Chongqing Gearbox Co., Ltd. – supplies gearboxes for wind turbines and industrial applications, ISO 9001, 10,000 units/year

strengths: Growing aerospace and EV supply chain

metal_cutting_machine_tools.description: Includes CNC lathes, milling machines, grinding machines, machining centers, and EDM. China is the world's largest machine tool producer and consumer.
growth_drivers: Aerospace demand for high-speed five-axis machines
Domestic substitution of mid-to-high-end CNC systems
metal_cutting_machine_tools.market_trends: Shift toward five-axis and multi-tasking machines; growing adoption of domestic CNC systems (GSK, Huazhong); retrofit market expanding.
metal_cutting_machine_tools.domestic_substitution_rate: 65% overall, 38% in five-axis and high-speed machines

construction_machinery.description: Excavators, wheel loaders, cranes, bulldozers, concrete machinery. China accounts for over 50% of global excavator sales.
growth_drivers: Green transformation (electric excavators, hydrogen fuel cells)
Export growth to Belt & Road countries
policy_support: National IV emission standards driving technology upgrade
Subsidies for electric construction machinery R&D
Export credit support for overseas projects
construction_machinery.domestic_substitution_rate: 95% for core machinery, 50% for high-end hydraulic components

agricultural_machinery.description: Tractors, harvesters, planters, irrigation systems, and drones. China aims for comprehensive mechanization of major crops by 2026.
agricultural_machinery.trends: Smart agriculture driving demand for GPS-guided tractors and autonomous harvesters; strong growth in high-horsepower models.

packaging_machinery.description: Filling machines, sealing machines, labeling machines, wrapping machines, and complete packaging lines.
packaging_machinery.domestic_substitution_rate: 85% in standard machines, 60% in high-speed/aseptic lines

industrial_robots.description: SCARA, six-axis, delta, collaborative robots, and automation cells.
policy_support: National key R&D program for core components (reducers, controllers)

supplier_evaluation_metrics.target: Clear capacity planning, typical lead time 30-60 days for standard machines
In-house R&D team, CAD/CAM/CAE tools, patents, ability to customize
≥3 years export, CE certification for EU, UL for US, compliance with target market standards
Global service network, remote diagnostics, spare parts availability

verification_methods: Request ISO certificates and product-specific certifications (CE, UL, CSA)
Conduct factory audit focusing on production processes, QC, and testing equipment
Review customer references and case studies (especially for similar applications)
Ask for sample machine test run (video or on-site) and performance data
Request dimensional inspection reports for key components
Check financial stability via credit reports (e.g., Sinosure report)
communication_best_practices: Provide detailed technical specifications: cycle time, precision, power, dimensions
Share 2D/3D drawings of parts to be machined (for machine tools) or product samples (for packaging lines)
Discuss acceptance criteria and commissioning plan upfront
Clarify warranty terms and spare parts availability
Establish a single point of contact with engineering background
sourcing_guide.payment_terms: Typical: 30% T/T deposit, 70% balance before shipment (or against scanned B/L). L/C at sight for large contracts. Some suppliers offer 30% deposit, 70% within 30 days after B/L with bank guarantee.

faq.question: What are typical MOQs for industrial machinery?
faq.answer: For standard machines (e.g., a CNC lathe), MOQ is usually 1 unit. For customized production lines, MOQ may be 1 line, but engineering and project management costs apply. Spare parts can be ordered in small quantities.

faq.question: Do Chinese machinery suppliers provide CE certification?
faq.answer: Many exporters offer CE certification as an option (usually at additional cost). Always request the CE declaration and technical file to ensure compliance with EU directives.

faq.question: Can I get customized machinery for my specific product?
faq.answer: Yes, most mid-to-large machinery companies have engineering teams for customization. Expect longer lead times and NRE fees. Provide detailed product drawings or samples for quotation.

faq.question: How do I ensure machinery meets my quality standards?
faq.answer: Conduct a factory acceptance test (FAT) before shipment, and a site acceptance test (SAT) after installation. Include performance test clauses in the contract (e.g., machining accuracy, cycle time).

faq.question: What is the best shipping method for heavy machinery?
faq.answer: For large machines, sea freight in containers (if fits) or break-bulk shipping. FOB terms are common. Suppliers usually arrange packaging suitable for ocean transport (waterproof, fumigation-free crates).

glossary.CNC: Computer Numerical Control – automated control of machining tools.
glossary.PLC: Programmable Logic Controller – industrial digital computer for automation.
glossary.Servo motor: Motor with closed-loop control for precise positioning.
glossary.Hydraulics: Use of pressurized fluid to transmit power (e.g., excavators).
glossary.Reducer: Gearbox that reduces speed and increases torque (critical for robots).
glossary.FAT: Factory Acceptance Test – testing at supplier's site before shipment.
glossary.SAT: Site Acceptance Test – testing after installation at buyer's facility.
glossary.CE: Conformité Européenne – mandatory conformity mark for machinery in EU.
glossary.OEM: Original Equipment Manufacturer – company that makes parts for others.
glossary.Turnkey: A complete solution delivered ready for operation.
